title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
After being assisted by a man with glasses, Kitcher arrives at the King's Head Inn. He interacts with the landlord, Victor Muggleton, who initially asks many questions about London. However, Muggleton’s demeanor shifts to cold contempt once Kitcher reveals he is a policeman from Scotland Yard.

Kitcher is shown to a simply decorated room, where he decides to disclose the reason for his visit to Greyladies to satisfy the landlord's scrutiny. He mentions the murders of Lady Siphe, Miss Gransberry, and Mr. Nigges at the Bluxton Hotel in London. Muggleton claims to know the victims but asserts they were neither friends nor enemies, stating they were merely people who "got into trouble." Kitcher senses suspicion in Muggleton's eyes and requests that the landlord keep his identity a secret. Muggleton dismissively responds that no one in Glebeholme will provide valuable information and suggests that if Kitcher wants to ask questions, he should do so in London.

Confused by the sudden change in hospitality, Kitcher goes downstairs to the bar. He observes two inebriated men: an elderly man with a white beard and a morose, muscular young man. After the young man stumbles out of the inn, the elderly man points at Kitcher and begins singing a song about a "London policeman," revealing that Kitcher's profession is no longer a secret. Kitcher confronts Muggleton about the breach of confidence, but Muggleton denies saying anything.
